title: Instant dashboard startup via persistent projection cache (T3Code pattern)

items.title: Non-blocking IssueDataService start
items.description: Remove await from startSharedIssueService() in main.ts. Split IssueDataService.start() to call loadCachedData() and pushSnapshot() immediately, then fire off API fetches in background without awaiting. The onIssuesChanged callback already wired in read-model.ts will handle incremental updates when fetches complete.
acceptance_criteria: Server starts and accepts WebSocket connections without waiting for API fetches
Initial getSnapshot RPC returns cached issue data (from SQLite L2 cache)
When API fetches complete, domain events update the read model and clients get incremental updates
No regression — if no cached data exists, first snapshot has empty issues until fetches complete

items.title: Persistent projection cache service
items.description: Create ProjectionCacheService that persists the full DashboardSnapshot to a projection_cache table in panopticon.db. Add the table schema to openEventDb(). Implement load() to read cached projection and save() with 100ms debounce to write on every event application.
acceptance_criteria: projection_cache table created in panopticon.db with key, data, sequence, updated_at columns
ProjectionCacheService.load() returns parsed DashboardSnapshot or null
ProjectionCacheService.save() upserts serialized snapshot with debounce
Corrupted JSON in cache is caught and returns null (fallback to fresh bootstrap)
No sync/blocking calls — all SQLite operations use the existing DbAdapter pattern

items.title: Read model bootstrap from projection cache
items.description: Modify ReadModelServiceLive bootstrap to try loading from ProjectionCacheService first. If a valid projection exists, use it as initial state and skip all lib module calls (agents, specialists, review statuses). Wire applyEvent to debounced-save the projection after every event. Fall back to existing bootstrap if no cache exists.
acceptance_criteria: On restart with populated cache: read model bootstraps from projection_cache in under 50ms
On restart with empty cache: falls back to existing lib-based bootstrap (no regression)
Every applyEvent triggers debounced projection save
Projection sequence tracks event store sequence correctly
If projection sequence < event store sequence, missing events are replayed on top of cached state

items.title: Client-side snapshot cache (localStorage SWR)
items.description: Create snapshotCache.ts with helpers to save/load DashboardSnapshot to localStorage (versioned key, 2MB cap, strip output buffers). Modify EventRouter to load cached snapshot on mount before WebSocket connects. Modify store to persist snapshot on every syncSnapshot. bootstrapComplete set to true from either cache or fresh data.
acceptance_criteria: On page load with cached snapshot: UI renders immediately from localStorage (before WebSocket connects)
bootstrapComplete is true from localStorage cache — skeletons disappear instantly on repeat visits
Cache is versioned (pan-snapshot-cache-v1) — old versions are ignored gracefully
Output buffers (agentOutputById, recentActivity) stripped before caching to stay under 2MB
Fresh snapshot from WebSocket overwrites stale cache cleanly

items.title: Freshness indicator component
items.description: Create FreshnessIndicator.tsx component that shows data staleness in the header. Shows 'Data from Xm ago' when rendering stale cache, 'Updating...' while waiting for fresh data, and 'Just now' when fresh snapshot arrives (fades after 5s). Add to dashboard header/layout.
acceptance_criteria: Shows relative timestamp ('Data from 2m ago') when rendering stale cached data
Shows 'Updating...' with subtle pulse when WebSocket connected but fresh snapshot not yet received
Shows 'Just now' when fresh data arrives, fades after 5 seconds
Positioned in header bar, non-intrusive, uses existing design system colors
Updates reactively from Zustand store timestamp
